prevalence.value: 0.5-1.5% (any cause; varies by season and setting)
prevalence.source: Hematology and infectious disease epidemiology syntheses
prevalence.trend: stable; seasonal increases with dengue outbreaks

burden.mortalityRate: Low overall; higher in severe secondary causes and bleeding

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Platelets <150,000/µL with evaluation of smear and secondary causes; bleeding risk assessment

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: CBC used in hospitals and prenatal care; no population screening
publicHealthSystem.estimatedPatients: very large transient burden during outbreaks
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Variable; depends on cause and access to specialist care and transfusion services

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: CBC widely used in febrile illness evaluation; specialized hematology workups concentrated in tertiary centers
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Supportive care in dengue; treat underlying causes; ITP management with steroids/IVIG; transfusion when indicated
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: High seasonal caseloads during dengue outbreaks and limited transfusion capacity in some districts
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Broad availability of CBC testing and expanding hospital services
